description: §8.5.1 UPDATE + RETURNING (RUNTIME, sqlEngine:"real") — a write returns the mutated row. bump(id, by) runs `UPDATE items SET qty = qty + ${by} WHERE id = ${id} RETURNING id, qty`.all(); the real Bun.SQL engine applies the arithmetic (qty = 5 + 3) to the WHERE-matched row and RETURNING yields {id:1, qty:8}. The stub returns [] for an UPDATE (no FROM match) and never evaluates the SET arithmetic — so the assertion passes ONLY on the real engine.
rationale: §8.5.1: a RETURNING clause read via `.all()` surfaces the written row (Bun.SQL executes UPDATE ... RETURNING natively — verified). Fork B loose-infer: items(id INTEGER, qty INTEGER) is inferred from the single seed row {id:1, qty:5}; no <schema> is required because there is no constraint under test. The `${by}`/`${id}` bound params + the `qty = qty + ${by}` arithmetic are all real-engine work the stub cannot express (empirically the stub yields []).
